aeon: noun. Commonwealth standard spelling of eon.
ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
ant: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Antakarinya.
ante: noun. A price or cost, as in up the ante.
antique: adjective. Having existed in ancient times, descended from antiquity; used especially in reference to Greece and Rome.
ate: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Atemble.
atone: verb. To make reparation, compensation, amends or satisfaction for an offence, crime, mistake or deficiency.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
equation: noun. The act or process of equating two or more things, or the state of those things being equal (that is, identical).
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
iota: noun. The ninth letter of the Greek alphabet (Ι, ι).
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
neat: adjective. Clean, tidy; free from dirt or impurities.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
oat: noun. Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
oaten: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or made of oats.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
qat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for reserved for local use.
qua: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Quapaw.
quaint: adjective. Of a person: cunning, crafty.
quean: noun. A woman, now especially an impudent or disreputable woman; a prostitute.
quiet: adjective. With little or no sound; free of disturbing noise.
quinate: adjective. Featuring five leaflets growing from a single point; quinquefoliolate.
quinoa: noun. A goosefoot (Chenopodium quinoa) native to the Andes and cultivated for its edible seeds.
quint: noun. An interval of one fifth.
quinta: noun. A country house in Madeira.
quinte: noun. The fifth defensive position, with the sword hand held at waist height, and the tip of the sword at knee height.
quit: adjective. Released from obligation, penalty, etc; free, clear, or rid.
quite: adverb. To the greatest extent or degree; completely, entirely.
quoin: noun. Any of the corner building blocks of a building, usually larger or more ornate than the surrounding blocks.
quoit: noun. A flat disc of metal or stone thrown at a target in the game of quoits.
quota: noun. A proportional part or share; the share or proportion assigned to each in a division.
quote: noun. A statement attributed to a person; a quotation.
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
tau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Upper Tanana.
tea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Temiar.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
toea: noun. A unit of currency, equivalent to one hundredth of a Papua New Guinean kina.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
toque: noun. A type of hat with no brim.
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
unite: verb. To bring together as one.
uta: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Otank.
